title: AI Tools Tier List for Agencies 2026
voiceScript: Most agencies in 2026 are paying for AI tools that belong in the trash tier. There are thousands now, and every week a new one promises everything. So you keep subscribing, keep switching, and nothing sticks. So I ranked the tools that actually run an agency this year, from daily drivers down to the ones you can skip. I sorted 20 tools across 4 tiers, and only 5 earned the top spot. Your edge was never the tool. It's the small stack you commit to and master. Stop collecting tools and start compounding one stack. DM list and I will send you the list. Follow for more.

scenes.voiceText: Most agencies in 2026 are paying for AI tools that belong in the trash tier.

scenes.headline: How they ranked

scenes.voiceText: Stop collecting tools and start compounding one stack. DM list and I will send you the list. Follow for more.
scenes.cta: DM 'list' for the full tier list

source: Infographic: 'AI Tools Tier List for Agencies 2026'. S-tier: ChatGPT v2, Manus, Claude, Codex, Claude Code. A-tier: Cursor, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Kling 3.0, Airtable, Nano Banana Pro 2. B-tier: n8n, Clay, Openclaw, Notion, Manychat. C-tier: Grok, Gamma, Chatdoc, Synthesia, Deepseek. 20 tools, 4 tiers, 5 per tier. Accompanying source article on the 2026 AI landscape: separating apps from models; generalist models (GPT 5.2, Gemini 3.1 Pro w/ 2M context, Claude Opus 4.6 w/ 1M context + non-sycophantic tone, Grok 4 real-time X); open-source/local movement (Llama 4 up to 400B params + 10M context, DeepSeek R1 cheap reasoning, Qwen 3.5, Kimi K2.5); aggregators (Perplexity/Sonar); generative modalities (Midjourney, Flux, Sora 2, Runway, Kling, Suno V5); and the shift to AI agents ('like interns', need oversight). Reel angle: agencies are drowning in tools; commit to a small S-tier stack instead of chasing the new thing.
